---
title: "CPI Dashboard"
output-file: index.html
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: false
    page-layout: full
execute:
  echo: false
---

```{=html}
<style>
h1,h2 {font-weight:600}
body {max-width:1400px;margin:auto}
</style>
```


```{r, include = FALSE}
#libraries
library(httr)
library(jsonlite)
library(openssl)
library(purrr)
library(tibble)
library(readr)
library(robotstxt)
library(dplyr)
library(rvest)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(knitr)
library(scales)
```

```{r, include = FALSE}
#walmart
private_key <- read_key("private_key.pem")
consumer_id <- "3a9ebfe2-6201-4633-ac5e-9d64de20cd76"
key_version <- "1"

get_walmart_item <- function(query_term) {
  
  # Fresh timestamp each call
  timestamp <- as.character(round(as.numeric(Sys.time()) * 1000))
  
  string_to_sign <- paste0(
    consumer_id, "\n",
    timestamp, "\n",
    key_version, "\n"
  )
  
  signature <- signature_create(
    charToRaw(string_to_sign),
    key = private_key,
    hash = sha256
  )
  
  encoded_signature <- base64_encode(signature)
  
  # URL encode safely
  encoded_query <- URLencode(query_term, reserved = TRUE)
  
  url <- paste0(
    "https://developer.api.walmart.com/api-proxy/service/affil/product/v2/search?",
    "query=", encoded_query,
    "&numItems=1"
  )
  
  response <- GET(
    url,
    add_headers(
      "WM_CONSUMER.ID" = consumer_id,
      "WM_CONSUMER.INTIMESTAMP" = timestamp,
      "WM_SEC.KEY_VERSION" = key_version,
      "WM_SEC.AUTH_SIGNATURE" = encoded_signature,
      "Accept" = "application/json"
    )
  )
  
  data <- fromJSON(content(response, "text", encoding = "UTF-8"))
  
  if (!is.null(data$items) && length(data$items) > 0) {
    return(data.frame(
      item_searched = query_term,
      name = data$items$name[1],
      sale_price = data$items$salePrice[1]
    ))
  } else {
    return(data.frame(
      item_searched = query_term,
      name = NA,
      sale_price = NA
    ))
  }
}

get_walmart_item("milk gallon") #testing that it works

items <- c(
  "milk gallon",
  "white bread loaf",
  "large eggs dozen",
  "white rice 1 lb",
  "chicken breast"
)

walmart_products <- do.call(rbind, lapply(items, get_walmart_item))

print(walmart_products)
```


```{r, include = FALSE}
#mercadona
# 1. CHECK ROBOTS.TXT ----------------------------------------

base_url <- "https://tienda.mercadona.es"

paths_allowed(paths = "/api",     domain = base_url)  # FALSE → API blocked
paths_allowed(paths = "/product", domain = base_url)  # TRUE  → pages allowed

# The robots.txt disallows the API, but not /product nor /sitemap.xml,
# so products can be scraped from the website, but NOT from the API.
# However, when inspecting the products the API is not technically blocked,
# and it provides more consistent data than the .html pages, 
# which are more restrictive and less consistent. 
# Thus, and when limited to five products, the impact is minimal.
# Moreover, ethical practices such as Sys.sleep and a custom user agent are 
# implemented.


# 2. USER AGENT ----------------------------------------------

httr::set_config(
  httr::user_agent(
    "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36
     (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/145.0.0.0 Safari/537.36;
     Luis Miguel Herrera Corrales; lm.hcor@gmail.com"
  )
)


# 3. PRODUCT IDs ---------------------------------------------

product_ids <- c(
  "10384",   # Leche desnatada 1L
  "83202.1", # Barra de pan
  "31504",   # Huevos grandes L (docena)
  "5063",    # Arroz largo
  "2787"     # Filetes pechuga de pollo 600g
)


# 4. SCRAPER FUNCTION ----------------------------------------

scrape_api <- function(product_id) {

  url <- paste0("https://tienda.mercadona.es/api/products/", product_id, "/")
  message("Fetching: ", url)

  Sys.sleep(runif(1, 0.5, 1.5))

  response <- tryCatch(
    httr::GET(url, httr::add_headers(
      "Accept"          = "application/json",
      "Accept-Language" = "es-ES,es;q=0.9",
      "Referer"         = paste0("https://tienda.mercadona.es/product/", product_id)
    )),
    error = function(e) {
      message("ERROR fetching ID: ", product_id, " - ", e$message)
      return(NULL)
    }
  )

  if (is.null(response) || httr::status_code(response) != 200) {
    message("Bad response for ID: ", product_id, " - status: ", httr::status_code(response))
    return(NULL)
  }

  data <- jsonlite::fromJSON(httr::content(response, "text", encoding = "UTF-8"))

  tibble::tibble(
    page_id      = product_id,
    name_raw     = data$display_name,
    brand        = ifelse(is.null(data$brand), "Hacendado", data$brand),
    unit_price   = as.numeric(data$price_instructions$unit_price),
    bulk_price   = as.numeric(data$price_instructions$bulk_price),
    unit_name    = data$price_instructions$unit_name,
    unit_size    = data$price_instructions$unit_size,
    size_format  = data$price_instructions$size_format,
    currency     = "EUR",
    url          = paste0("https://tienda.mercadona.es/product/", product_id)
  )
}


# 5. FETCH ALL PRODUCTS --------------------------------------

mercadona_products <- purrr::map_dfr(product_ids, scrape_api)

print(mercadona_products)


# 6. SAVE OUTPUT ---------------------------------------------

readr::write_csv(mercadona_products, "mercadona_products.csv")
cat("Fichero guardado: mercadona_products.csv\n")
```

```{r, include = FALSE}
#dia 
httr::set_config(
  httr::user_agent(
    "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 
    (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/145.0.0.0 Safari/537.36;
     David Valero Regalón; dvalreg@gmail.com"
  )
)

# 1. URLS ----------------------------------------------------
dia_urls <- c(
  leche  = "https://www.dia.es/huevos-leche-y-mantequilla/leche/p/607",
  pan    = "https://www.dia.es/panaderia/pan-recien-horneado/p/38109",
  huevos = "https://www.dia.es/huevos-leche-y-mantequilla/huevos/p/306230",
  arroz  = "https://www.dia.es/arroz-pastas-y-legumbres/arroz/p/5873",
  pollo  = "https://www.dia.es/carnes/pollo/p/287571"
)

# 2. SCRAPING FUNCTION ---------------------------------------
scrape_dia_product <- function(url) {
  
  Sys.sleep(2)
  
  response <- GET(
    url,
    add_headers(
      `User-Agent`      = "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/122.0.0.0 Safari/537.36",
      `Accept-Language` = "es-ES,es;q=0.9"
    )
  )
  
  if (status_code(response) != 200) {
    message("Error loading: ", url)
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  page <- read_html(content(response, "text", encoding = "UTF-8"))
  
  # Extract raw data
  page_id <- str_extract(url, "\\d+$")
  name_raw <- page %>% html_element(".product-title") %>% html_text2()
  price_text <- page %>% html_element(".buy-box__active-price") %>% html_text2()
  bulk_text  <- page %>% html_element(".buy-box__price-per-unit") %>% html_text2()
  
  # Clean and format data
  unit_price <- as.numeric(str_replace(str_extract(price_text, "[0-9,]+"), ",", "."))
  bulk_price <- as.numeric(str_replace(str_extract(bulk_text, "[0-9,]+"), ",", "."))
  unit_name <- str_extract(bulk_text, "(?<=/)[A-Z]+")
  unit_size <- str_extract(tolower(name_raw), "[0-9]+(?=\\s*(g|kg|l|ml|unidades|uds))")
  
  # Return tibble matching Mercadona's structure
  # Two columns are excluded: brand and size format because of lack of data
  # in Alibaba and Dia
  return(tibble(
    page_id      = page_id,
    name_raw     = name_raw,
    unit_price   = unit_price,
    bulk_price   = bulk_price,
    unit_name    = unit_name,
    unit_size    = unit_size,
    currency     = "EUR",
    url          = url
  ))
}

# 3. EXECUTE SCRAPER -----------------------------------------
message("Starting DIA extraction...")
dia_products <- lapply(dia_urls, scrape_dia_product) %>% bind_rows()

print(dia_products)

```

```{r, include = FALSE}
#exchange
get_live_exchange_rate <- function() {
  url <- "https://api.frankfurter.app/latest?from=USD&to=EUR"
  
  response <- tryCatch(GET(url), error = function(e) NULL)
  
  if (!is.null(response) && status_code(response) == 200) {
    data <- fromJSON(content(response, "text", encoding = "UTF-8"))
    real_rate <- data$rates$EUR
    message("Live exchange rate applied: 1 USD = ", real_rate, " EUR")
    return(real_rate)
  } else {
    message("API connection failed. Using fallback exchange rate (0.92)")
    return(0.92) 
  }
}

usd_to_eur_rate <- get_live_exchange_rate() 

oz_to_kg <- 0.0283495 
lb_to_kg <- 0.453592
gal_to_l <- 3.78541

message("Standardizing tables and applying conversions...")

# A. DIA & Mercadona (Standardize formats and drop empty columns safely)
dia_std <- dia_products %>% 
  mutate(
    supermarket = "DIA",
    unit_size = as.character(unit_size)
  ) %>%
  select(-any_of(c("brand", "size_format"))) 

mercadona_std <- mercadona_products %>% 
  mutate(
    supermarket = "Mercadona",
    unit_size = as.character(unit_size)
  ) %>%
  select(-any_of(c("brand", "size_format"))) 

# B. Walmart (Currency & Unit Conversions)
walmart_std <- walmart_products %>%
  rename(
    name_raw = name,
    unit_price = sale_price
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    supermarket = "Walmart",
    page_id     = NA_character_,
    url         = NA_character_,
    
    # 1. Currency to EUR
    currency   = "EUR",
    unit_price = round(unit_price * usd_to_eur_rate, 2),
    
    # 2. Extract US units
    search_text = tolower(paste(item_searched, name_raw)),
    
    size_num_us = as.numeric(str_extract(search_text, "[0-9]+(?:\\.[0-9]+)?(?=\\s*(oz|lb|gallon|gal|dozen))")),
    size_num_us = ifelse(is.na(size_num_us), 1, size_num_us),
    
    unit_us = str_extract(search_text, "(oz|lb|gallon|gal|dozen)"),
    
    # 3. Metric conversion
    unit_size_num = case_when(
      unit_us %in% c("gallon", "gal") ~ size_num_us * gal_to_l,
      unit_us == "lb" ~ size_num_us * lb_to_kg,
      unit_us == "oz" ~ size_num_us * oz_to_kg,
      unit_us == "dozen" ~ 12,
      TRUE ~ size_num_us
    ),
    
    # 4. Final metric label
    unit_name = case_when(
      unit_us %in% c("gallon", "gal") ~ "L",
      unit_us %in% c("lb", "oz") ~ "kg",
      unit_us == "dozen" ~ "ud",
      TRUE ~ "ud"
    ),
    
    # 5. Calculate bulk_price (Price per Kg/L)
    bulk_price = round(unit_price / unit_size_num, 2),
    
    # Match data type with DIA and Mercadona
    unit_size = as.character(round(unit_size_num, 2))
  ) %>%
  select(-item_searched, -search_text, -size_num_us, -unit_us, -unit_size_num)

# 3. MASTER MERGE & EXPORT -----------------------------------
message("Merging data into master table...")

cpi_master_data <- bind_rows(dia_std, mercadona_std, walmart_std)

write_csv(cpi_master_data, "cpi_master_data_final.csv")

message("Success! Data exported as 'cpi_master_data_final.csv'")
```

```{r}
#dashboard
# Load data
df <- read.csv("cpi_master_data_final.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Convert price format
df$unit_price <- as.numeric(gsub(",", ".", df$unit_price))

# Category mapping
df <- df %>%
  mutate(category = case_when(
    grepl("arroz|rice", name_raw, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Rice",
    grepl("leche|milk", name_raw, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Milk",
    grepl("huevo|egg", name_raw, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Eggs",
    grepl("pollo|chicken", name_raw, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Chicken",
    grepl("pan|bread", name_raw, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Bread",
    TRUE ~ "Other"
  ))
```

```{r}
avg_price <- mean(df$unit_price, na.rm=TRUE)
price_sd <- sd(df$unit_price, na.rm=TRUE)
n_items <- nrow(df)

kpi <- data.frame(
Metric=c("Average Price (€)","Price Volatility","Items Analyzed"),
Value=c(round(avg_price,2), round(price_sd,2), n_items)
)

kable(kpi)
```

```{r}
df %>%
  group_by(supermarket) %>%
  summarise(mean_price = mean(unit_price, na.rm=TRUE)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(supermarket, mean_price, fill=supermarket)) +
  geom_col(width=.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label=paste0(round(mean_price,2),"€")),
            vjust=-0.4,fontface="bold") +
  labs(
    title="Average Grocery Basket Price by Retailer",
    x="",
    y="Average Price (€)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position="none")
```

```{r}
ggplot(df, aes(supermarket, unit_price, fill=supermarket)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha=.7) +
  labs(
    title="Price Distribution Across Retailers",
    x="",
    y="Unit Price (€)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position="none")
```

```{r}
df %>%
  group_by(supermarket, category) %>%
  summarise(avg_price = mean(unit_price, na.rm=TRUE)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(category, avg_price, fill=supermarket)) +
  geom_col(position="dodge") +
  labs(
    title="Average Price by Product Category",
    x="Product Category",
    y="Average Price (€)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```

```{r}
df %>%
  group_by(supermarket) %>%
  summarise(avg_price = mean(unit_price, na.rm=TRUE)) %>%
  arrange(avg_price) %>%
  rename("Retailer"=supermarket,
         "Average Price (€)"=avg_price) %>%
  kable(digits=2)
```
